flashcards.front: What is a binary relation on a set A?
flashcards.back: A binary relation R on set A is a subset of the Cartesian product A × A, i.e., R ⊆ A × A. It consists of ordered pairs (a, b) where a and b are elements of A.

flashcards.front: When is a relation R on set A called reflexive?
flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is reflexive if every element is related to itself, i.e., ∀a ∈ A, (a, a) ∈ R. Example: The relation '≤' on integers is reflexive since a ≤ a for all integers a.

flashcards.front: When is a relation R on set A called symmetric?
flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is symmetric if whenever (a, b) ∈ R, then (b, a) ∈ R for all a, b ∈ A. Example: The relation 'is married to' on people is symmetric.

flashcards.front: When is a relation R on set A called antisymmetric?
flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is antisymmetric if (a, b) ∈ R and (b, a) ∈ R implies a = b for all a, b ∈ A. Example: The relation '≤' on real numbers is antisymmetric since a ≤ b and b ≤ a implies a = b.

flashcards.front: When is a relation R on set A called transitive?
flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is transitive if (a, b) ∈ R and (b, c) ∈ R implies (a, c) ∈ R for all a, b, c ∈ A. Example: The relation 'is ancestor of' on people is transitive.

flashcards.front: What is an equivalence relation? Give an example.
flashcards.back: An equivalence relation is a relation that is simultaneously reflexive, symmetric, and transitive. Example: Congruence modulo n (a ≡ b mod n) on integers is an equivalence relation; it partitions integers into equivalence classes.

flashcards.front: What is a partial order relation?
flashcards.back: A partial order (or poset) is a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive. Example: The subset relation '⊆' on the power set of a set is a partial order.

flashcards.front: How do you verify if a relation is an equivalence relation or a partial order?
flashcards.back: To verify an equivalence relation, check all three properties: reflexive, symmetric, and transitive. For a partial order, check reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive. Any property failing means the relation is neither.
